headerDisplay:
Homebridge plugin for the Pima FORCE alarm panel.

The alarm panel dials *out* to this plugin: configure a CMS path on the panel (Installer Code → System Configuration → CMS & Communication → Monitoring Station → CMS2/3 → Comm.Paths → Network) pointing to this Homebridge host on the port below, with Protocol set to JSON and Account ID matching the value here.

name.title: Plugin Name
name.description: How the plugin appears in the Homebridge log.

port.title: TCP Port
port.description: Port the alarm panel will dial in to. Must match the CMS path port configured on the panel.

account.title: Account ID
account.description: Account ID configured on the panel's CMS path. Distinct from any other CMS (e.g., Control4).

encoding.title: Panel Text Encoding
encoding.description: Character encoding the panel uses for zone and user names. Default `windows-1255` is correct for Israeli FORCE panels with Hebrew names. Switch to `utf-8` for English-only installations or non-Hebrew panel localizations. Anything the standard `TextDecoder` accepts is valid (`utf-8`, `windows-1255`, `iso-8859-1`, `iso-8859-2`, etc.). If your zone names appear as `?` or replacement characters in HomeKit, the encoding is wrong.

debug.title: Debug Logging
debug.description: When on, every JSON frame received from or sent to the alarm panel is logged at info level (passwords are redacted). Useful for diagnosing issues; noisy in normal operation.

siren.title: External Siren (Switch)
siren.description: Optional Switch accessory mirroring the external siren state. ON = siren currently sounding; OFF = silent. Toggle OFF while sounding to mute the siren. Cannot be turned ON manually.

enabled.title: Expose siren accessory
enabled.description: Off to suppress the siren accessory entirely.

userCode.title: User Code
userCode.description: User code for arming/disarming this partition. Stored in plain text in Homebridge config — protect access to the host accordingly.

armModes.title: Enabled Arm Modes
armModes.description: Uncheck modes you don't use to remove them from the HomeKit picker. DISARM is always available.

zones.title: Zones
zones.description: Each zone on the panel exposed as a HomeKit sensor. The plugin auto-populates this list on first connect by querying zone names from the panel; you only need to set the sensor type. New zones added to the panel later are appended automatically.

name.title: Display Name
name.description: Shown in HomeKit (e.g., 'Front Door', 'Living Room Motion').

type.title: Sensor Type
type.description: How this zone is exposed in HomeKit. The panel-side semantics are identical (active/restored); the type only affects the HomeKit icon and which automation primitives it offers.
